channel.title: Ghosxt Blog — Managed IT & Cybersecurity for the Central Coast
channel.description: Practical IT, cybersecurity, and compliance guidance for small businesses on California's Central Coast, from a DoD-cleared IT engineer.

item.title: The Microsoft Teams "IT Help Desk" That Isn't: A Ransomware Playbook
item.dc:creator: Ulises Paiz
item.description: Attackers are flooding inboxes with spam, then messaging staff on Microsoft Teams while posing as the IT help desk, talking them into Quick Assist remote access, and dropping ransomware. How the email-bombing-plus-Teams-vishing playbook works and how a small business shuts it down, from a DoD-cleared engineer.

item.title: Still on Windows 10 in Mid-2026? What a Small Business Should Do Now
item.dc:creator: Ulises Paiz
item.description: Windows 10 support ended October 14, 2025, and the consumer ESU lifeline expires in October 2026. A plain-language guide for small businesses: who can upgrade to Windows 11 free, who needs new PCs, what ESU really costs, and how to plan it without panic, from a DoD-cleared engineer.

item.title: How to Protect Your Small Business From a Cyberattack: 10 Essentials
item.dc:creator: Ulises Paiz
item.description: A no-jargon checklist for owners: the 10 things that actually protect a small business from a cyberattack in 2026, in priority order, what each one stops, and how to tell if yours is really in place, from a DoD-cleared engineer on California's Central Coast.

item.title: Nightmare-Eclipse: The 6 Windows Zero-Days From the Banned Researcher
item.dc:creator: Ulises Paiz
item.description: GitHub and GitLab banned the researcher Nightmare-Eclipse for dropping six unpatched Windows zero-days in six weeks. Here is what BlueHammer, RedSun, UnDefend, YellowKey, GreenPlasma, and MiniPlasma actually do, which are patched, and what a small business should do this week, from a DoD-cleared engineer.

item.title: FBI Warns: Silent Ransom Group Is Targeting Law Firms. What to Do
item.dc:creator: Ulises Paiz
item.description: The FBI warns that the Silent Ransom Group (Luna Moth) is targeting law firms with phone-based social engineering, fake IT support, and data-theft extortion. What law firms and professional-services SMBs on the Central Coast should do, from a DoD-cleared engineer.

item.title: MFA Fatigue: When the Attacker Just Asks Your Team for the Code
item.dc:creator: Ulises Paiz
item.description: Attackers are not stealing the second factor anymore; they are getting your employees to hand it over. How MFA fatigue, push bombing, and help-desk social engineering work, and the SMB controls that actually stop them. From a DoD-cleared engineer.

item.title: Cybersecurity Services in Monterey: What Your Small Business Should Actually Buy in 2026
item.dc:creator: Ulises Paiz
item.description: A plain-talk read on what a Monterey small business should buy for cybersecurity in 2026: identity, endpoint, monitoring, email, training, and what to skip. From a DoD-cleared engineer who runs cybersecurity programs across Monterey, Pacific Grove, Carmel, and Salinas.

item.title: Trucking and Logistics IT in Salinas: What Owner-Operators and Small Fleets Should Have in Place
item.dc:creator: Ulises Paiz
item.description: A plain-talk IT checklist for trucking and logistics businesses in Salinas, Watsonville, and Gilroy. ELDs, dispatch, compliance, cybersecurity, and the patterns that actually work for a 1-30 truck operation. From a DoD-cleared engineer.

item.title: OneDrive vs SharePoint vs Teams Files: Which Should Your Small Business Use?
item.dc:creator: Ulises Paiz
item.description: A plain-talk decision guide on when to use OneDrive, SharePoint, or Teams files for a 15-100 person business. Permissions, governance, and the patterns that actually work, from a DoD-cleared engineer.

item.title: Backup and Disaster Recovery for a Small Business: What Actually Works in 2026
item.dc:creator: Ulises Paiz
item.description: Most SMB backup plans look fine on paper and fall apart in a real incident. A DoD-cleared engineer's plain-talk read on what works in 2026: the 3-2-1-1-0 rule, immutable storage, recovery testing, and ransomware-aware design.

item.title: Microsoft 365 Copilot Business: Lock In $18 Before July 1, 2026
item.dc:creator: Ulises Paiz
item.description: Microsoft 365 Copilot Business locks in at $18 per user per month if you buy before June 30, 2026; after that the standard rate is $21. A plain-talk read on whether your SMB should buy now, from a DoD-cleared engineer.

item.title: WordPress Funnel Builder Compromise: What Every SMB With a Website Should Check
item.dc:creator: Ulises Paiz
item.description: Active exploitation of a Funnel Builder plugin flaw is injecting card-skimming JavaScript into WooCommerce checkouts. Plain-talk verification steps for SMB site owners from a DoD-cleared engineer.

item.title: SharePoint Server RCE CVE-2026-33112: Patch This Week
item.dc:creator: Ulises Paiz
item.description: CVE-2026-33112 is an authenticated remote code execution flaw in on-prem SharePoint Server 2016, 2019, and Subscription Edition, fixed in the May 2026 Patch Tuesday cumulative update. Plain-talk guidance for SMBs from a DoD-cleared engineer.

item.title: Windows MiniPlasma SYSTEM Privilege Escalation: No Patch, Public PoC
item.dc:creator: Ulises Paiz
item.description: A working PoC for a Windows Cloud Filter driver privilege-escalation bug (CVE-2020-17103) is on GitHub. Standard user to SYSTEM on fully patched Windows 11. Microsoft's 2020 patch did not fully fix the underlying issue, per the researcher. Plain-talk defensive guidance for SMBs from a DoD-cleared engineer.

item.title: AI Has Cut Attacker Handoff to 22 Seconds: What an SMB Should Do
item.dc:creator: Ulises Paiz
item.description: Adversary handoff from initial access to a second threat actor has dropped from 8 hours to 22 seconds in three years. Human-paced security operations cannot keep up. Plain-talk guidance for small business from a DoD-cleared engineer.

item.title: Top 5 Website Design Companies in Salinas & Monterey Bay (2026)
item.dc:creator: Ulises Paiz
item.description: An honest comparison of five real website design companies serving Salinas, Monterey, Santa Cruz, San Benito, and Santa Clara counties in 2026: Ghosxt, Monterey Premier, Mag One Media, Peakify Marketing, and Zestful Media & Design. Public-info only; Ghosxt is on the list with bias disclosed upfront.

item.title: Top 5 Managed IT Services Providers in Salinas & Monterey Bay (2026)
item.dc:creator: Ulises Paiz
item.description: An honest comparison of five real managed IT services providers serving Salinas, Monterey County, Santa Cruz County, San Benito County, and Santa Clara County (San Jose, Gilroy) in 2026: Ghosxt, SRS Networks, Alvarez Technology Group, Pinnacle Technology Services, and Adaptive Information Systems. Public-info only, with bias disclosed upfront.

item.title: Switching IT Providers: A Checklist for Salinas & Monterey Small Businesses
item.dc:creator: Ulises Paiz
item.description: A practical, owner-tested checklist for switching managed IT providers in Salinas, Monterey, Santa Cruz, San Benito, and Santa Clara counties (including San Jose and Gilroy): how to know it's time, what you own vs what your MSP owns, the access-recovery sequence to run BEFORE giving notice, and a 30/14/0-day transition timeline.

item.title: PG&E PSPS Preparedness: IT Continuity Plan for Central Coast & South Bay Small Businesses
item.dc:creator: Ulises Paiz
item.description: A practical IT continuity plan for PG&E Public Safety Power Shutoff events in Monterey County, Santa Cruz County, San Benito County, and Santa Clara County. UPS sizing, generators, cellular failover, cloud-first architecture, and what to do in the 48-hour notice window.

item.title: IT Support vs. Managed IT Services: What's the Difference?
item.dc:creator: Ulises Paiz
item.description: A plain-English guide to the difference between IT support, managed IT services, break-fix, MSPs, MSSPs, helpdesk, and co-managed IT. What each one actually means, what it costs, and which one a small business in Salinas, Monterey, Santa Cruz, or Santa Clara County actually needs.

item.title: IT Help, Help Desk & Tech Support for Small Businesses in Salinas
item.dc:creator: Ulises Paiz
item.description: Need IT help, a help desk, break-fix support, or just someone to call when the printer dies? Ghosxt is the local Salinas option for small business IT support across Monterey, Santa Cruz, San Benito, and Santa Clara counties. Founder-led, fast response, flat-rate pricing.

item.title: How Much Does Managed IT Cost in Salinas in 2026?
item.dc:creator: Ulises Paiz
item.description: A plain-talk pricing guide for managed IT services in Salinas, Monterey County, Santa Cruz County, San Benito County, and Santa Clara County (San Jose, Gilroy): the four pricing models MSPs use, real 2026 ranges ($125-$250 per user per month), what should be included, and the hidden line items to watch for. Includes Ghosxt's actual published rates.

item.title: HIPAA-Compliant IT for Medical & Dental Practices in Monterey County
item.dc:creator: Ulises Paiz
item.description: What HIPAA actually requires of small medical and dental practices in Monterey County, Santa Cruz County, San Benito County, and Santa Clara County: the four IT pillars (identity, encryption, audit, backup), BAA basics, EHR considerations, common compliance gaps, and what it costs to do this right.

item.title: Cyber Insurance Renewal Checklist for Central Coast Small Businesses (2026)
item.dc:creator: Ulises Paiz
item.description: What cyber insurance carriers actually require in 2026: MFA, EDR, immutable backups, patch cadence, security awareness training, incident response plan. The 12 questions on every modern application and how a Salinas, Monterey, Santa Cruz, or Santa Clara County small business should prepare for renewal.

item.title: Best IT Support Options for Small Businesses in Salinas, CA
item.dc:creator: Ulises Paiz
item.description: A plain-talk guide to IT support options for small businesses in Salinas, Monterey, Santa Cruz, San Jose, and Gilroy: in-house, break-fix, managed services, and hybrid. What to look for, what each path actually costs, and how Central Coast and South Bay buyers should evaluate it. From a DoD-cleared engineer.

item.title: Exchange Server Zero-Day CVE-2026-42897: What to Do Today
item.dc:creator: Ulises Paiz
item.description: Microsoft confirmed active exploitation of CVE-2026-42897, an OWA cross-site scripting zero-day in on-prem Exchange Server 2016, 2019, and Subscription Edition. CVSS 8.1, no permanent patch yet, EEMS mitigation already applying. Plain-talk guidance from a DoD-cleared engineer.

item.title: May 2026 Patch Tuesday: What to Patch This Week
item.dc:creator: Ulises Paiz
item.description: Microsoft's May 2026 Patch Tuesday fixed about 120 flaws, 17 of them critical, with no zero-days. The Word use-after-free (CVE-2026-40361) and the Windows DNS Client heap overflow (CVE-2026-41096) are the two SMBs should patch first. Plain-talk read on what to do this week.

item.title: Identity Hardening for the 5-Employee Company
item.dc:creator: Ulises Paiz
item.description: The minimum identity controls that actually move the needle for a 5-employee business in 2026: phishing-resistant MFA, Conditional Access, no persistent local admins, separated admin accounts, and legacy auth turned off. From a DoD-cleared engineer.

item.title: YellowKey & GreenPlasma: Two Windows Bypasses Explained
item.dc:creator: Ulises Paiz
item.description: A plain-talk defender's read on YellowKey (BitLocker bypass via WinRE on Windows 11 and Server 2022/2025) and GreenPlasma (CTFMON privilege escalation). How they work, what's at risk, and what small businesses should actually do this week.

item.title: Claude Mythos: 271 Firefox Bugs in One Run
item.dc:creator: Ulises Paiz
item.description: Anthropic's Claude Mythos found 271 Firefox 150 vulnerabilities in one eval and built working exploits for 181. What the patch-cadence shift means for SMBs.
